case_title: JESSIE EVANS VS. THE UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al

docket_entries.description: POS OF OPPOSITION TO PLTF'S EX PATE APPLICATION; DECLARATION OF MICHAEL J VARTAIN; STIP AND PROPOSED ORDER CONTINUING HEARING FILED BY DEFENDANT THE UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO GORE-MANN, DEBRA VARTAIN, MICHAEL
DECLARATION OF J. EVANS IN SUPPORT OF REPLY TO DFS' OPPOSITION TO PL'S MTN TO LIFT DISCOVERY STAY FILED BY PLAINTIFF EVANS, JESSIE

PLAINTIFF JESSIE EVANS' MOTION TO LIFT DISCOVERY STAY IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE WHAT FACTS PLAINTIFF EXPECTS TO DISCOVER THAT ARE NOT OTHERWISE WITHIN PLAINTIFF'S KNOWLEDGE OR A PART OF THE RECORD. THE VARTAIN DECLARATIONS SPECIFICALLY STATE WHAT HE KNEW AND RELIED UPON. FURTHER, THE RECORD REFLECTS THAT PLAINTIFF CONSENTED TO GORE-MANN'S STATEMENT REGARDING A LEAVE AND THE ALLEGATION IN THE COMPLAINT ABOUT PRIOR MISCONDUCT IS VAGUE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: KIMBERLY JENNINGS, CSR 11452

DECLARATION (SUPPLEMENTAL) OF MICHAEL J. VARTAIN IN SUPPORT OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE FILED BY DEFENDANT THE UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO GORE-MANN, DEBRA VARTAIN, MICHAEL J.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE UNIVERSITY OF S.F., DEBRA GORE-MANN & MICHAEL VARTAIN'S MOTION TO STRIKE. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE APPLIES TO THE DEFAMATION CLAIM, COUNTS 5 AND 6. EVANS IS A PUBLIC FIGURE; THE COMMENTS AT ISSUE RELATE TO THAT POSITION, AND AS TO THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION THE MOVING PARTIES HAVE ESTABLISHED THE FIRST PRONG. MCGARRY V. UNIV. OF SAN DIEGO (2007) 154 CAL. APP. 4TH 97, 115. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED AN ABILITY TO PREVAIL ON THESE CLAIMS. THE RECORD REFLECTS PLAINTIFF'S AGREEMENT WITH THE "LEAVE OF ABSENCE" REFERENCE AND SO THIS IS NOT ACTIONABLE UNDER ROYER V. STEINBERG (1979) 90 CAL. APP. 3D 490, 498. NEITHER THE COMPLAINT NOR PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE SETS FORTH WHAT MS. GORE-MANN STATED REGARDING MISCONDUCT AT PLAINTIFF'S PRIOR EMPLOYMENT. AS TO MR. VARTAIN'S STATEMENTS, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE OF MALICE AS REQUIRED UNDER MCGARRY AT 114. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR IT IS ALLEGED ONLY AGAINST DOES. THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION CENTER ON PLAINTIFF'S HIRING, EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP AND TERMINATION. THE CLAIMS EMBRACE FAR MORE THAN PROTECTED CONDUCT AND FOR THAT REASON, DEFENDANT'S HAVE NOT MET THE FIRST PRONG OF CCP 425.16. GIVEN THE RULING ON THE FRAUD CLAIM, THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE COURT FOUND THE TWO DEFENDANTS, HAVING PREVAILED ON THE MOTION, THAT FEES OF $13,000.00 IS A REASONABLE AMOUNT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Jul-30-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS THE UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DEBRA GORE-MANN AND MICHAEL VARTAIN'S MOTION TO STRIKE

Appearances:
Michael Vartain of the Vartain Law Group (391.1155) for defendants and moving parties
Dan Siegel of Siegel & Yee (510.839.1200) for plaintiff and moving party

Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: granted in part and denied in part. The anti-SLAPP statute applies to the defamation claim, counts 5 and 6. Evans is a public figure; the comments at issue relate to that position, and as to those causes of action the moving parties have established the first prong. McGarry v. Univ. of San Diego (2007) 154 Cal. App. 4th 97, 115. Plaintiff has not demonstrated an ability to prevail on these claims. The record reflects plaintiff's agreement with the "leave of absence" reference and so this is not actionable under Royer v. Steinberg (1979) 90 Cal. App. 3d 490, 498. Neither the complaint nor plaintiff's evidence sets forth what Ms. Gore-Mann stated regarding misconduct at plaintiff's prior employment. As to Mr. Vartain's statements, plaintiff has presented no evidence of malice as required under McGarry at 114. The motion is denied as to the 4th cause of action for it is alleged only against Does. The second and third causes of action center on plaintiff's hiring, employment relationship and termination. The claims embrace far more than protected conduct and for that reason, defendant's have not met the first prong of CCP 425.16. Given the ruling on the fraud claim, the motion is denied as to punitive damages. The Court found the two defendants, having prevailed on the motion, that fees of $13,000.00 is a reasonable amount. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Lift Discovery Stay
Off Calendar Jul-09-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JESSIE EVANS' MOTION TO LIFT DISCOVERY STAY

Appearances: Dean Royer of Siegel & Yee (510-839-1200) for plaintiff and moving party
Michael Vartain of the Vartain Law Group (391-1155) for defendant and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion is denied. Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate what facts plaintiff expects to discover that are not otherwise within plaintiff's knowledge or a part of the record. The Vartain declarations specifically state what he knew and relied upon. Further, the record reflects that plaintiff consented to Gore-Mann's statement regarding a leave and the allegation in the complaint about prior misconduct is vague. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Kimberly Jennings, CSR 11452
